scope.feature: Permission-mode parity (default / acceptEdits / bypassPermissions + allow/deny/ask rules)
scope.whatItIs: Adds a session-level permission MODE plus seedable allow/deny/ask rules on top of juno's existing per-call permission gate, ported as a SUBSET of Claude Code's permission system. Three modes: `default` (today's behavior — safe tools auto-allow, risky/dangerous prompt), `acceptEdits` (file edits/writes additionally auto-allow without prompting), and `bypassPermissions` (everything auto-allows except matching deny rules). The allow/deny/ask rules are config-seeded permission patterns (`allow`/`deny`/`ask` lists) that feed the SAME `policy.evaluate` matcher juno already runs for remembered patterns. Mode + rules live in juno's local `PermissionPolicyOptions` and resolve to the EXISTING three `evaluate` returns (`auto-allow`/`auto-deny`/`prompt`) and the EXISTING four `PermissionDecision` values — no new decision type. On the claude-cli render-only backend the CLI runs its OWN permission system natively under `claude -p`, so juno does NOT re-implement it there; juno either passes the mode through as `--permission-mode <m>` or suppresses its own gate on that backend (a key decision). The gate change is concentrated in `permissions/policy.ts` + `permissions/patterns.ts` (juno-local, NOT the frozen seam), with optional UI wiring for a mode chip + `/permissions` toggle.

valueToJuno.summary: Real ergonomic win — acceptEdits removes the per-edit prompt fatigue during code iteration, and allow/deny rules let a project pre-grant trusted tools. But juno already ships the load-bearing safety primitive (the interactive gate + remembered always-allow patterns), so this is a convenience/parity layer, not a capability unlock. bypassPermissions is the security-negative half and is mostly only meaningful in isolated containers. Medium value: nice for daily driving, not essential.

effort.rationale: The core policy change is genuinely small and additive (a `mode` field + a tool-category notion + branch in the risk fallback, all juno-local). What pushes it from S to M: (1) acceptEdits CANNOT be expressed by RiskLevel alone — `write_file`/`edit_file` AND `spawn_subagent` are all `risky`, so the policy needs a new way to distinguish 'edit-class' tools from other risky tools (a tool-category tag or an edit-tool allow-set), which touches the Tool assembly and every tool's metadata OR adds a name allow-list in the policy; (2) the render-only split forces a real decision + claude-cli buildArgs change (pass-through `--permission-mode` vs suppress) that can only be confidence-checked against a live `claude -p`; (3) allow/deny/ask rule parsing adds 4-5 touches in config.ts plus the env-overlay enum whitelist; (4) optional UI (mode chip, /permissions overlay, mode persistence across the session) is its own slice. None of it is algorithmically hard, but it spans policy + config + cli + claude-cli + UI, and the CLI-backend half is not fake-verifiable.

drivers: bypassPermissions DELETES the interactive permission gate — SECURITY.md names that gate as one of three foundational guarantees; a blanket bypass mode must be an explicit, user-driven opt-in or it silently removes the guardrail (and it propagates to every subagent via the shared policy instance).

acceptEdits cannot be expressed by RiskLevel — naive 'auto-allow all risky' would ALSO auto-allow spawn_subagent (risky), over-granting. Needs a tool-category distinction the codebase does not currently have.

Render-only split: on claude-cli the CLI runs its own permission-mode natively; if juno ALSO sets a mode on its (suppressed) gate, the two can disagree. Mode on claude-cli is only correct as pass-through via --permission-mode, which is NOT fake-verifiable.
The shared policy instance means a mode set once silently propagates to ALL subagents — intended for default/acceptEdits, but bypass-in-a-subagent is a sharper edge.

seamsTouched.change: Add `mode?: 'default'|'acceptEdits'|'bypassPermissions'` (and optionally a `setMode`) to the juno-local `PermissionPolicyOptions`; branch in the risk fallback so bypass returns auto-allow for all non-denied calls and acceptEdits auto-allows edit-class tools. Seed allow/deny/ask rules via the existing `initial[]` plus an `ask` concept. All resolves to the existing 3 returns.
Optionally extend salient-key extraction beyond path/dir if rules must distinguish tools by a non-path arg; allow/deny/ask glob matching already works as-is. Pure, juno-local.

Add `permissionMode` enum + `permissions: {allow/deny/ask}` to Settings (4-5 touch: interface, DEFAULT_SETTINGS, parseSettings with enum whitelist, mergeSettings, optional JUNO_PERMISSION_MODE env override).
Pass `mode` + seeded rules into `createPermissionPolicy({...})` from settings (one shared instance, already the wiring point at line 63).
DECISION-GATED: if pass-through is chosen, add `--permission-mode <m>` to buildArgs so the CLI's native gate honors the mode. If suppress is chosen, no change. Not fake-verifiable.

OPTIONAL UI slice: a `mode:<m>` chip fed via selectStatusLine context (no reducer change) and/or a /permissions slash toggle. If mode is session-mutable via the reducer that is an additive-optional event — frozen-seam additive only.

keyDecisions: RENDER-ONLY SPLIT — on the claude-cli backend, does juno PASS THROUGH the mode as `--permission-mode <m>` (CLI honors it natively, juno's gate stays inert there) or SUPPRESS (let the CLI use its own default, juno sets nothing)? Pass-through gives parity but is unverifiable by fakes; suppress is safe but means mode only affects raw-API backends. Recommend: pass-through for default/acceptEdits, but treat bypass on the CLI with extra care.

acceptEdits MECHANISM — since RiskLevel can't distinguish edit-tools from other risky tools, do you (a) add an explicit edit-tool name allow-set inside the policy (e.g. {write_file, edit_file}), (b) add a tool-category/`editsFiles` tag to the Tool metadata, or (c) seed acceptEdits as concrete allow-patterns for the edit tool names? Option (a) is smallest and keeps the frozen Tool contract untouched.

Do you ship bypassPermissions AT ALL in v1? It removes the SEC-foundational gate and propagates to subagents. Options: ship it gated behind an explicit env/flag opt-in, ship it as UI-only with a loud indicator, or CUT it for v1 and ship only default+acceptEdits (the safe, high-value subset).

ALLOW/DENY/ASK rules — is `ask` a genuinely new outcome (force-prompt even for safe tools) or just 'not-allowed'? `ask` maps to the existing `prompt` return, so it's expressible — but it inverts autoAllowSafe for matched safe tools. Confirm `ask` must override safe-auto-allow.

Is the mode SESSION-MUTABLE (Shift-Tab/`/permissions` toggle, needs session state that survives turns) or CONFIG-ONLY (read once from settings at startup)? Config-only is zero UI/frozen-seam cost; session-mutable needs app state and possibly an additive reducer event.

RULE SPECIFIER GRAMMAR — Claude Code uses `Tool(specifier)` like `Bash(npm test)`. juno has no bash tool (SEC: no shell) and its matcher salient is path/dir only. Do you adopt CC's `Tool(arg)` grammar (needs a parser + richer salient extraction) or keep juno's existing `name:path` glob grammar for the seeded rules? Keeping juno's grammar is far cheaper.
buildSketch: Pin the decision matrix first: confirm modes shipped (default + acceptEdits, bypass yes/no), the acceptEdits mechanism (edit-tool name set vs Tool tag), the rule grammar (juno `name:path` glob vs CC `Tool(arg)`), and the claude-cli stance (pass-through vs suppress). These four forks gate everything downstream.

Extend the juno-local `PermissionPolicyOptions` with `mode` and seeded allow/deny/ask rules; keep `evaluate` returning ONLY the existing three values. Add the mode branch to the risk fallback: bypass -> auto-allow unless a deny rule matched; acceptEdits -> auto-allow for the edit-tool set, otherwise fall through to today's behavior.

Implement the edit-tool distinction (the chosen mechanism) so acceptEdits does NOT accidentally auto-allow spawn_subagent or other risky non-edit tools. Keep deny-wins precedence ahead of any mode auto-allow.

Thread allow/deny/ask seeding through the existing `initial[]`/rules path so config-provided rules feed the same matcher as remembered patterns; ensure deny-first precedence holds across seeded + remembered rules.

Add `permissionMode` + `permissions.{allow,deny,ask}` to config.ts (interface, defaults, parse with enum whitelist, merge, optional env override), then wire them into the single `createPermissionPolicy(...)` call in cli.ts so the one shared instance carries the mode to the executor AND every subagent.
Handle the claude-cli backend per the pinned stance: if pass-through, add `--permission-mode <m>` to buildArgs; if suppress, document that mode is raw-API-only and leave buildArgs untouched.

OPTIONAL UI slice: add a `mode:<m>` StatusLine chip via selectStatusLine context (no reducer touch); if session-mutable, add a `/permissions` toggle + app state (and an additive-optional reducer event only if mode must be reducer-owned).

Write vitest coverage for the raw-API/policy half: default unchanged, acceptEdits auto-allows edits but still prompts spawn_subagent, bypass auto-allows but deny rules still win, allow/deny/ask precedence (deny > ask > allow), and subagent inheritance of the mode via the shared instance.
LIVE-VERIFY the claude-cli half (if pass-through): run a real `claude -p` with `--permission-mode acceptEdits` and confirm the CLI honors it and juno's render is unaffected — fakes cannot prove this.
Run THE GATE: `npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run` must stay green; confirm no frozen-seam (events/reducer/contracts) edit was needed beyond at most one additive-optional field.
dependencies: Shares the seeded-rules path with any future allow/deny work for MCP tools (patterns.ts salient extraction) — if MCP lands later, the richer salient key benefits both.

Coupled to the still-open OQ on the claude-cli native permission posture (PORT_SPEC OQ 11 / render-only split). The pass-through stance here should be consistent with how hooks/MCP pass-through is decided on the CLI backend.
If mode is made session-mutable, depends on the App-level session state pattern (same place a future plan-mode would have lived — but plan mode is CUT, so no conflict).
No dependency on Unit 3, hooks, or Managed Agents; can ship independently.
openRisks: acceptEdits mis-scoping: if implemented as 'auto-allow all risky' it silently over-grants spawn_subagent and any future risky non-edit tool. The edit-tool distinction MUST be explicit or this is a latent privilege-escalation.

bypassPermissions propagation: the shared policy instance means bypass set once flows into every subagent turn automatically. Intended for the parent, but a bypass-mode subagent running unattended is a sharper guardrail removal than the parent prompt-fatigue case it was meant to solve.

Render-only divergence: if juno sets a mode on its (suppressed) claude-cli gate while the CLI also runs its own default mode, the user sees one mode in the chip but a different mode actually enforced. Only pass-through avoids this, and pass-through is not fake-verifiable.

ask-vs-safe interaction: an `ask` rule that must override autoAllowSafe inverts the default safe-tool behavior; if the precedence (deny > ask > allow > risk-fallback) isn't implemented exactly, safe tools could slip through unprompted or trusted tools could start prompting.

SECURITY posture drift: SECURITY.md treats the interactive gate as load-bearing and states a dangerous call is NEVER auto-allowed by risk alone. bypassPermissions contradicts that invariant by construction; shipping it without a SEC.md posture update leaves the doc and code inconsistent.
Test-count/doc drift already flagged in the maps (260 vs 269) is unrelated but means the gate baseline should be re-confirmed before claiming green.

verdict.feature: Permission-mode parity (default / acceptEdits / bypassPermissions + allow/deny/ask rules)
verdict.effortCorrection: M is right, but for a reason the draft under-weights, not the reasons it lists. The single biggest cost driver is the claude-cli pass-through, which the draft mis-scopes as a localized buildArgs change touching no frozen seam. In reality buildArgs (claudeCliClient.ts:265-280) reads ONLY from TurnInput + ModelEntry; TurnInput lives in the FROZEN core/contracts.ts. So pass-through forces an additive-optional `permissionMode?` on TurnInput (frozen seam) PLUS threading through useStreamingTurn.ts (input literal at :271-278 + the useCallback dep array at :297-309) PLUS AppDeps + app.tsx. That is a 4-file thread the draft never lists. If bypass+acceptEdits is config-only (no UI) and pass-through is CUT (suppress on claude-cli), it is a clean S/low-M. With pass-through it is a solid M. Not L.
seamErrors: MISSING SEAM — TurnInput (C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/core/contracts.ts:30-42) is not in seamsTouched at all, yet the recommended claude-cli pass-through stance cannot reach buildArgs without it. buildArgs (claudeCliClient.ts:265-280) derives its arg vector exclusively from TurnInput (id/model/effort/cwd/systemPrompt) and ModelEntry. To emit `--permission-mode <m>` the mode MUST ride on TurnInput. The draft's claudeCliClient seam entry says 'add --permission-mode to buildArgs' as if the value is already in scope there — it is not. This is the load-bearing omission.

WRONG touchesFrozenSeam — the draft marks the claudeCliClient change touchesFrozenSeam:false. The buildArgs edit itself is juno-local, but its PRECONDITION (a permissionMode on TurnInput) edits the FROZEN core/contracts.ts. Additive-optional, so it's allowed under the additive-only rule, but the draft's claim that pass-through needs 'at most one additive-optional field' on events/reducer is in the wrong file: it is TurnInput in contracts.ts, NOT events/reducer, and the draft never names it.

HALLUCINATED `ask` primitive — the draft repeatedly treats `ask` as seedable via 'the existing initial[] plus an ask concept' and asserts 'ask maps to the existing prompt return, so it's expressible.' FALSE as stated. policy.ts stores only deny / always-allow-pattern / dangerous-bypass (StoredDecision = Exclude<PermissionDecision,'allow-once'>, policy.ts:21). There is NO rule decision that yields 'prompt' on match. An `ask` rule that force-prompts a matched SAFE tool (overriding autoAllowSafe) is NOT expressible by the current rule map — it needs a genuinely NEW StoredDecision variant ('ask'/'force-prompt') AND a new branch in the evaluate match loop (policy.ts:48-61) AND, because PermissionDecision is a FROZEN events.ts union, either a new frozen-union member (cascades into the never-switches in policy.ts:88-101, executor.ts:89-94, and eventToAction) OR a policy-internal rule-decision type kept OUT of PermissionDecision. The draft calls ask 'expressible — confirm precedence'; it is actually a net-new mechanism, not a precedence tweak. This is under-scoped.

INACCURATE patterns.ts framing — the draft says allow/deny/ask glob matching 'already works as-is.' deny and allow globs do work as-is via matchesPattern. ask does not exist (see above). And the salient key is path/dir-only (patterns.ts:48-62), so any seeded rule that must discriminate a tool by a non-path arg silently collapses to the empty salient and over-matches — the draft flags this only as 'optional/if rules must distinguish', understating that juno's own tools (grep keys on dir, spawn_subagent has no path at all) already can't be rule-targeted by their salient arg.
hiddenTraps: RENDER-SPLIT double-enforcement, sharpened: on claude-cli the executor never runs (juno only renders the CLI stream), so juno's policy.mode is INERT there regardless. The only way mode affects claude-cli is pass-through. But pass-through to `claude -p --permission-mode bypassPermissions` hands FULL bypass to the native CLI gate — and that is NOT fake-verifiable AND it is the exact 'silently removes the SEC-foundational gate' hole, now executed by a subprocess juno does not gate. The draft notes 'treat bypass on the CLI with extra care' but does not register that on claude-cli bypass is enforced 100% by the CLI, with juno having zero residual gate to fall back on. There is no juno safety net on that backend by construction.

acceptEdits MUST be a name allow-set, confirmed by the code: write_file and edit_file are risk:'risky' (fileTools.ts:297,330) and spawn_subagent is ALSO risk:'risky' (subagentTool.ts:107). A naive 'acceptEdits = auto-allow risky' in the evaluate risk-switch would auto-allow spawn_subagent — privilege escalation into a nested unattended turn whose own nested awaitPermission is hard-wired to 'deny' (subagentTool.ts:174) but which still RUNS. The draft's option (a) edit-tool name set is therefore not just 'smallest' — it is the only correct option, and it must live as an explicit {write_file,edit_file} set checked BEFORE the risk switch.

Shared-policy propagation is structurally guaranteed, not optional: there is exactly ONE policy instance (cli.ts:63) passed to BOTH the executor and SubagentDeps.policy (cli.ts:82 -> subagentTool.ts:39,166-175). A mode set via a mutating setMode() on that instance propagates to every in-flight and future subagent with no opt-out seam. For default/acceptEdits benign; for bypassPermissions this means a bypass set in the parent makes every spawned subagent run risky edit tools unattended. The draft flags this but treats it as an 'edge'; it is the default behavior of the one-instance design and there is no per-subagent mode override anywhere in SubagentDeps.

config.ts enum whitelist is a real 5th touch the draft counts but the env-overlay path (applyEnvOverrides:178-205) has NO enum-validating template — maxContext uses a numeric guard; a permissionMode env override needs a brand-new string-allowlist guard or JUNO_PERMISSION_MODE=garbage silently poisons the mode. Minor but it's a parse-validation slot that doesn't exist yet.
verdict.notes: The draft is directionally sound and its big-ticket risk calls (acceptEdits can't use RiskLevel; bypass removes the SEC gate; shared-policy propagation; pass-through not fake-verifiable) are all CONFIRMED against the real code. The policy.ts/patterns.ts/config.ts/cli.ts seams are real and correctly located, and the gate baseline is genuinely 269/269 green (re-ran vitest). It earns 'sound' on the raw-API half. It needs revision on three concrete code-grounded points: (1) the claude-cli pass-through stance it RECOMMENDS silently requires an additive-optional permissionMode field on the FROZEN TurnInput (contracts.ts) plus a 4-file thread (useStreamingTurn input literal + dep array, AppDeps, app.tsx) that seamsTouched omits entirely and mislabels as touchesFrozenSeam:false — the frozen touch is in contracts.ts, not events/reducer; (2) the `ask` rule is presented as already-expressible but the rule store (StoredDecision, policy.ts:21) has no decision that yields 'prompt' on match, so ask is a net-new mechanism (new internal rule-decision + new evaluate branch), not a precedence confirmation; (3) acceptEdits as an explicit {write_file,edit_file} name set is the only correct mechanism (not merely 'smallest'), because spawn_subagent shares risk:'risky' with the edit tools. Recommend: CUT bypassPermissions for v1 (ship default+acceptEdits, the safe high-value subset), make mode CONFIG-ONLY to dodge session-state/reducer cost, and either CUT claude-cli pass-through (suppress; document mode = raw-API-only) to avoid the TurnInput frozen-seam thread and the unverifiable-bypass hole, or scope that TurnInput addition explicitly as its own line item. All file paths cited are absolute and verified by direct read.
verdict.riskAdjustment: Hold at medium, but re-weight the drivers. The draft's stated drivers are correct. Add two the draft under-weights: (a) on the claude-cli backend, bypassPermissions pass-through is enforced ENTIRELY by the native CLI with zero juno residual gate (the executor never runs there) — so 'bypass on CLI' is not 'extra care', it is total delegation of the SEC-foundational guarantee to an unverifiable subprocess; (b) the `ask`-overriding-autoAllowSafe path requires a new rule-decision the current store cannot represent, so an under-implemented ask is not just a precedence bug — it is a missing primitive that could let a safe tool slip through unprompted. Net risk stays medium ONLY if bypass is cut and pass-through is either cut or explicitly scoped; if both bypass AND claude-cli pass-through ship in v1, risk rises to medium-high because the one genuinely un-fake-verifiable, gate-removing path is also the one the maps say repeatedly bit Wave 2.

scope.feature: Managed Agents eval — richer agent management beyond Wave-3 spawn_subagent
scope.whatItIs: Two distinct things hide under one label and the scope must keep them apart. (A) The DECISION half — an explicit go/no-go on the Anthropic Managed Agents API (the hosted, beta, NON-Messages API with coordinator rosters, multiagent, session threads, and a first-class skills[] field). PORT_SPEC mandates this be "weighed, not dismissed" (PORT_SPEC:104,236c, OQ6 PORT_SPEC:227). It is a decision doc, NOT code. (B) The BUILD half — incrementally richer management of juno's OWN already-shipped spawn_subagent tool (src/tools/subagentTool.ts) + agent definitions (src/services/agents.ts): parse more of the .claude/agents/*.md frontmatter that juno currently DROPS (today only name/description/prompt/model/tools survive — agents.ts:58-70), add per-agent permission scoping, a /agents listing affordance, and richer nested rendering. Verified ground truth: spawn_subagent is raw-API-only with a depth-1 structural guarantee (registry.ts:29-34 snapshots childTools before pushing spawn), shared policy, nested awaitPermission→'deny' (subagentTool.ts:174). The Anthropic Managed Agents API does NOT fit ModelClient.streamTurn (contracts.ts:95-97) — it is a different API, so it can only enter as a NEW provider backend, never as a tweak to the existing seam.

valueToJuno.summary: Low-to-moderate. The DECISION half (Anthropic Managed Agents API) is genuinely valuable as a one-time written go/no-go that the docs explicitly require — but the honest answer is almost certainly NO-GO for v1: it is a different non-Messages API, beta, hosted-execution (runs on Anthropic infra OUTSIDE juno's workspace jail, SEC:60-83), and juno already shipped the portable spawn_subagent that PORT_SPEC:227 chose as the v1 answer. The BUILD half (richer frontmatter parsing, per-agent permission scoping, /agents listing) is real but incremental polish on a feature that already works; nobody is blocked on it. Highest-value concrete slice is parsing permissionMode/disallowedTools/maxTurns from agent frontmatter so agent definitions actually scope their sub-agents — but that is gated on permission-mode parity landing first.

effort.rationale: Split by half. The DECISION half is S — a few hours of writing a grounded go/no-go doc (no code, no gate). The BUILD half is M and decomposes: (1) extend AgentDefinition + frontmatter parsing for the dropped fields (disallowedTools/maxTurns/skills/effort) — small, additive, fully fake-testable; (2) per-agent permission scoping (disallowedTools as a deny-list, permissionMode per agent) — medium, but BLOCKED on permission-mode parity existing as a concept first, and the nested awaitPermission→'deny' floor caps how much a per-agent mode can even do; (3) a /agents listing slash command + chip — small UI, mirrors the skills chip; (4) richer nested rendering reuses Unit-3's parentToolUseId additive field, so it is effectively a dependent of Unit 3, not independent work. The full hosted-API integration (if ever greenlit) would be XL — a whole new provider, hosted execution, MCP+vault auth — but that is explicitly OUT of this eval's scope (eval = decide, not build).

drivers: SCOPE AMBIGUITY is the top risk: 'Managed Agents eval' conflates the hosted Anthropic API (decision-only) with richer juno-side agent management (build). Treating them as one thing produces either an over-built hosted integration nobody greenlit or a decision doc that ships no value. Must split first.

The hosted Managed Agents API is a DIFFERENT API, not Messages — it cannot ride ModelClient.streamTurn (contracts.ts:95-97). Any real integration is a new provider backend + a hosted-execution trust boundary OUTSIDE the workspace jail (SEC:60-83) + MCP+vault auth (PORT_SPEC:99). High security surface; the eval should recommend defer.

Per-agent permission scoping is entangled with permission-mode parity (a separate Wave-4 item). disallowedTools is a clean additive deny-list, but permissionMode per agent has no meaning until juno has modes; and the nested awaitPermission→'deny' floor (subagentTool.ts:174) means a per-agent bypassPermissions could let a sub-agent run risky tools with NO UI gate — a guardrail-removal risk that must be designed deliberately, not inherited by accident.

Richer nested rendering depends on Unit 3 (the parentToolUseId additive field on the FROZEN seam) and on a live multi-subagent claude -p stream to verify — so that slice inherits Unit 3's live-verify gate and frozen-seam caution.

seamsTouched.change: Extend AgentDefinition + discoverInRoot to parse the dropped frontmatter fields (disallowedTools, maxTurns, skills, effort, permissionMode). The parser already has the machinery: parseScalars for scalars, extractList for lists (frontmatter.ts). Additive, fully fake-testable.

Consume the new agent fields: disallowedTools → subtract from childTools in selectChildTools (currently only an allow-list intersect, subagentTool.ts:91-101); maxTurns → cap the nested runTurn loop; permissionMode → influence the child executor's policy. Module-level factory pattern, no frozen-contract change.
No change needed for richer agent fields (they flow via the existing subagent.agents dep). Only touched if a new managed-agent tool variant is added — not recommended for this eval.

Per-agent permissionMode needs the policy to be mode-aware (shared dep with the separate permission-mode-parity item). Keep modes re-mapping onto the existing three returns (auto-allow|auto-deny|prompt) to avoid a frozen-contract touch.
Optional /agents listing slash command + an agents:{N} chip, mirroring the existing skills:{N} chip template. AppDeps/StatusLineState additions kept optional.

ONLY if richer nested-agent rendering is in scope: the additive-optional parentToolUseId? on streaming/tool AgentEvent variants + ToolState — the SAME field Unit 3 introduces. eventToAction is an exhaustive switch with no default, so even an additive variant forces an edit there. Additive-only is achievable; this is the lone frozen-seam contact and it is shared with Unit 3.

ONLY if the hosted Anthropic Managed Agents API were ever greenlit (NOT recommended in this eval): a new provider string + BUILTIN_MODELS entry + a new createModelClient branch. Additive but large; explicitly out of scope for the eval.

keyDecisions: SPLIT FIRST: is this deliverable the DECISION (go/no-go doc on the hosted Anthropic Managed Agents API) or the BUILD (richer juno-side agent management), or both? They are different work with different gates. Recommended: produce the decision doc (it is required by PORT_SPEC and is cheap) and recommend NO-GO on the hosted API for v1; pick at most one small build slice.

If building: which slice? (a) parse-more-frontmatter (smallest, additive, independent); (b) per-agent permission scoping (blocked on permission-mode parity); (c) /agents listing UI (small, independent); (d) richer nested rendering (depends on Unit 3 + live-verify). Recommended: (a) and/or (c) only — both are independent, additive, fake-testable.

Per-agent permissionMode semantics given the nested awaitPermission→'deny' floor: does a per-agent bypassPermissions actually let a sub-agent run risky/dangerous tools with no UI gate? If yes, that is deliberate guardrail removal and needs an explicit security decision; if no, permissionMode on an agent is near-inert today and parsing it is cosmetic. Recommended: parse it but treat it as documentation-only until interactive nested agents exist.

RENDER-ONLY SPLIT for agent management on claude-cli: the CLI auto-discovers .claude/agents/ natively and juno passes NO --agents (buildArgs only sets -p/--output-format/--model/--effort, claudeCliClient.ts:265-280). Do richer juno-side agent fields apply ONLY to the raw-API spawn_subagent path, with the CLI backend left to its native behavior? Recommended: YES — never pass --agents (double-define trap, same as the skills double-load guard); juno-side fields govern only spawn_subagent.

Does richer nested rendering get folded into Unit 3 (shared parentToolUseId field, shared live-verify gate) or scoped separately? Recommended: fold into Unit 3 — they touch the same frozen-seam field and the same live claude -p stream.
buildSketch: Disambiguate the deliverable: write the eval/decision doc that explicitly weighs the Anthropic Managed Agents hosted API (different non-Messages API, beta, hosted execution outside the workspace jail, MCP+vault auth) against juno's already-shipped spawn_subagent, per PORT_SPEC:227/236c. Recommend NO-GO for v1 with reasons. This half ships NO code.

Inventory the frontmatter gap: confirm which CC agent fields juno currently DROPS (verified: agents.ts keeps only name/description/prompt/model/tools; it ignores permissionMode/maxTurns/skills/disallowedTools/mcpServers/hooks/effort/background/color).

Pick the one or two independent, additive build slices worth doing now (recommended: parse disallowedTools + maxTurns + skills + effort into AgentDefinition; optionally an /agents listing slash command + chip). Defer everything entangled with permission-mode parity or Unit 3.
Extend AgentDefinition and discoverInRoot to read the chosen extra fields using the existing parseScalars/extractList helpers; keep all new fields optional.

Thread the new fields into spawn_subagent: turn disallowedTools into a deny subtraction in selectChildTools (it currently only does an allow-list intersect); apply maxTurns as a nested-loop cap; pass skills/effort into the nested TurnInput. All via the module-level factory — no contract change.
Add the optional /agents listing affordance (slash command + agents:{N} chip) mirroring the skills chip, feeding data via selectStatusLine context, not the reducer.

If (and only if) richer nested rendering is in scope: coordinate with Unit 3 to share the additive-optional parentToolUseId? field on the AgentEvent variants + ToolState, update the exhaustive eventToAction switch, and gate verification on a LIVE multi-subagent claude -p stream.
Run the gate (tsc --noEmit + vitest) green for every code slice; the frontmatter/permission-scoping slices are fully fake-testable, so they do NOT need live verification.
Write up the eval doc + any shipped slice; record the explicit NO-GO/defer recommendation on the hosted API and the security rationale.
dependencies: Per-agent permission scoping (permissionMode field) depends on the separate Wave-4 permission-mode-parity item existing as a concept first — without modes, per-agent permissionMode is inert.

Richer nested-agent RENDERING depends on Unit 3 (the additive parentToolUseId? field on the frozen events/reducer seam) and inherits Unit 3's live-verify requirement (a real multi-subagent claude -p stream; fakes will not catch interleave/index-collision bugs).

The hosted Managed Agents API integration (if ever greenlit — NOT recommended) would depend on a whole new provider backend, hosted-execution trust model, and MCP+vault auth — explicitly out of scope for the eval.
All code slices depend on the gate staying green: cd C:/Users/Core/src/juno && npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run (currently 269/269).
No new npm packages required for the build slices (frontmatter parsing is dependency-free in frontmatter.ts).
openRisks: The label 'eval' implies decision-only, but the docs also fold richer agent management (a code build) under the same Wave-4B umbrella — if the deliverable is not split up front, the work either over-builds a hosted integration nobody greenlit or produces a doc that ships zero user value.

The frontmatter-only slices ARE fully fake-testable and do NOT need live verification — but the richer-nested-rendering slice does (live claude -p, shared with Unit 3). liveVerifyNeeded is set true because at least one in-scope slice (nested render) cannot be proven by fakes; the independent slices can be.

Per-agent bypassPermissions colliding with the nested awaitPermission→'deny' floor (subagentTool.ts:174) is a latent guardrail-removal path: parsed-but-applied incorrectly, a sub-agent could run risky tools with no UI gate. Needs an explicit security decision before permissionMode is honored rather than just parsed.

The hosted Managed Agents API runs code on Anthropic infrastructure OUTSIDE juno's workspace jail and pairs with MCP+vault auth — adopting it would puncture the SEC spine (no-train + workspace-jail + permission-gate). The eval should recommend defer on security grounds, not just cost.

Doc drift: PORT_SPEC still lists Wave-4 as plan-mode(4A)+decision-doc(4B) and says 4B 'verifies a decision doc, not code', while the live scope folds the Unit-3 build into 4B. The eval should restate the actual split so reviewers are not misled by stale PORT_SPEC framing.

verdict.feature: Managed Agents eval — richer agent management beyond Wave-3 spawn_subagent
verdict.effortCorrection: M is correct and arguably conservative. DECISION half = S (doc only, no gate) is right. BUILD half: slice (1) parse-more-frontmatter is genuinely S-to-small — the existing parseScalars/extractList helpers in frontmatter.ts (verified) cover scalars (maxTurns/effort/permissionMode, with trivial numeric coercion for maxTurns) and lists (disallowedTools/skills) with no new parsing machinery; fully fake-testable. Slice (2) per-agent permission scoping is correctly flagged as blocked on permission-mode parity. The only place effort could creep is if reviewers conflate the eval with the XL hosted-API build — but the draft explicitly fences that as out of scope. No correction needed.
seamErrors: NO seam errors found. Every cited path/line verified against the real files: agents.ts:58-70 keeps only name/description/prompt/model/tools and DROPS the rest (confirmed lines 58-70); the def literal sets name/description/prompt/source, then conditionally model (64-66) and tools via extractList (67-70) — exactly as claimed. subagentTool.ts:174 awaitPermission: async () => 'deny' (confirmed verbatim). selectChildTools at 91-101 is an allow-list intersect only (filter + Set.has), no deny subtraction today — the draft's 'currently only an allow-list intersect' is accurate. registry.ts:29-34 snapshots childTools=[...tools] BEFORE pushing spawn_subagent (confirmed lines 29-33), the depth-1 structural guarantee. contracts.ts:95-97 ModelClient.streamTurn is the single Messages-shaped seam (confirmed). claudeCliClient buildArgs (265-280) sets ONLY -p/--output-format/--verbose/--include-partial-messages/--model/--effort — NO --agents (confirmed) and the three parent_tool_use_id drops are at 153-156/171-175/192-195 (grep confirmed). 269/269 gate confirmed by live vitest run.
hiddenTraps: INDEX-COLLISION trap is correctly identified but UNDER-credited as a dependency rather than owned: the draft folds 'richer nested rendering' into Unit 3 and inherits its live-verify gate — correct. But it should make explicit that the partition-by-parent_tool_use_id fix lives INSIDE claudeCliClient's Map<number, ToolAccumulator> (keyed by SSE index, where parent and child both start at index:0), NOT in the reducer (whose state.tools is keyed by the globally-unique tool_use_id string — verified reducer.ts:55,157,174 — so the reducer never collides). The draft's seamsTouched for events/reducer is right that the FROZEN-seam touch is only the additive parentToolUseId?, but it omits that the actual collision bug is an adapter-internal accumulator-partitioning fix. A scoper reading only this draft might think the additive field alone solves rendering; it does not.

eventToAction EXHAUSTIVE-SWITCH trap: the draft correctly notes eventToAction is exhaustive with no default (verified events.ts:59-86, no default case) so even an additive optional parentToolUseId? on existing variants forces touching that frozen file. CORRECTION to nuance: adding an OPTIONAL field to existing variants (text-delta/tool-call/etc.) does NOT by itself force an eventToAction edit (the field can be threaded through or ignored and still compiles) — the forced edit only happens if you want the field to actually FLOW to the Action. The draft slightly overstates 'even an additive variant forces an edit there' — that's true for a NEW variant, but parentToolUseId? is a new FIELD on existing variants, which compiles without an eventToAction change until you choose to propagate it. Minor, and the draft's additive-only conclusion still holds.

RENDER-ONLY SPLIT correctly handled: the draft's keyDecision that juno-side agent fields govern ONLY the raw-API spawn_subagent path and the CLI keeps native .claude/agents/ auto-discovery (never pass --agents — double-define trap) is verified correct against buildArgs (no --agents) and matches the skills double-load guard pattern. No double-work error here.
verdict.riskAdjustment: Risk level 'medium' is right. The dominant risk (scope ambiguity: decision-vs-build) is correctly named as #1. The security framing is sound and NOT overstated: hosted Managed Agents API runs outside the workspace jail and the eval correctly recommends NO-GO/defer on security grounds, not just cost. One risk to ELEVATE slightly: the per-agent bypassPermissions × nested-awaitPermission→'deny' floor interaction. Today the floor (subagentTool.ts:174) hard-denies every nested prompt, so a parsed permissionMode:bypassPermissions on an agent def is INERT — but the draft's recommendation to 'parse it but treat as documentation-only' is the safe call ONLY as long as that 'deny' floor stays hardcoded. If a later wave makes nested prompts interactive, a previously-parsed-and-stored bypassPermissions would silently activate a guardrail bypass. The eval should record this as a tripwire (do not honor permissionMode until the floor is deliberately redesigned), which the draft's openRisks already does. Adequately covered.
verdict.notes: The draft is genuinely solid and I could not break its load-bearing claims. Every file path, line cite, and signature I checked is accurate against the real code (agents.ts, subagentTool.ts, registry.ts, frontmatter.ts, policy.ts, events.ts, contracts.ts, claudeCliClient.ts, selectors.ts, StatusLine.tsx, reducer.ts, cli.ts), and the 269/269 gate is confirmed by a live vitest run. The central architectural judgments are correct: (1) the hosted Anthropic Managed Agents API does NOT fit ModelClient.streamTurn and can only enter as a new provider backend — verified; recommend NO-GO is the honest call. (2) The build half is real but incremental polish; the smallest independent slices (parse-more-frontmatter via existing parseScalars/extractList; /agents chip mirroring the verified skills:{N} chip at StatusLine.tsx:34-36) are additive, fake-testable, and touch no frozen seam. (3) The render-only split is respected — juno-side fields govern only spawn_subagent, CLI keeps native auto-discovery, never pass --agents. Three refinements (not errors): the actual index-collision fix is an adapter-internal accumulator partition keyed by parent_tool_use_id, not a reducer change (reducer keys by globally-unique tool_use_id so it never collides); adding an optional field to EXISTING AgentEvent variants compiles without an eventToAction edit until you choose to propagate it (a new VARIANT would force the edit); and permissionMode parsing stays safe only while the nested awaitPermission→'deny' floor at subagentTool.ts:174 remains hardcoded — flag that as a tripwire for any future interactive-nested wave. None of these change the verdict. The doc-drift callout (PORT_SPEC still frames 4B as decision-only while Unit-3 is a code build folded in) is accurate and worth restating in the eval so reviewers aren't misled. Relevant files (absolute): C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/services/agents.ts, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/tools/subagentTool.ts, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/tools/registry.ts, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/services/frontmatter.ts, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/permissions/policy.ts, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/core/events.ts, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/core/contracts.ts, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/core/reducer.ts, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/core/selectors.ts, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/ui/StatusLine.tsx, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/providers/claudeCliClient.ts, C:/Users/Core/src/juno/src/cli.ts.

name: Managed Agents eval (richer agent management beyond Wave-3 spawn_subagent)

scope.feature: Unit 3 — nested subagent activity render on the claude-cli (subscription) backend
scope.whatItIs: Today, when the subscription backend runs `claude -p --output-format stream-json`, the CLI itself spawns and runs nested subagents (native `.claude/agents/` auto-discovery). Every event those subagents emit carries a non-null `parent_tool_use_id`, and juno's claudeCliClient DROPS all three kinds at exactly claudeCliClient.ts:153-156 (consolidated `assistant` block), 171-175 (`stream_event` deltas), and 193-195 (`user` tool_result echo). So subagent activity is invisible in juno's transcript — the user sees the parent's `Agent`/`Task` tool card go pending→result with only the CLI's final summarized text, never the child's interim tool calls. Unit 3 stops dropping those events and instead ATTRIBUTES them: it threads the `parent_tool_use_id` through as an additive-optional `parentToolUseId?` on the streaming/tool AgentEvent variants, the matching reducer Actions, and the `Block`/`ToolState` shapes, so the reducer can file child tool cards under their parent and the renderer (Message.tsx → ToolCallCard) can draw them indented/nested beneath the parent Agent tool. This is render-and-attribution only — juno still never re-executes anything the CLI ran (the render-only split holds; `cliStopReason` still maps `tool_use`→`end`).

valueToJuno.summary: Genuine UX win for the PRIMARY (subscription/claude-cli) backend: multi-agent runs become legible instead of an opaque pending-then-summary. But it is the single highest-effort-per-payoff Wave-4 item because it is the ONLY one touching the frozen seam AND the only one whose correctness cannot be proven by vitest fakes — it needs a live multi-subagent `claude -p` stream (the exact trap that produced both Wave-2 bugs). It is also render-only on a backend that already works; nothing is broken today (subagent work is summarized into the CLI's final answer — fine for v1). Medium value, real but not load-bearing.

effort.rationale: Three distinct surfaces, two of them delicate. (1) FROZEN-SEAM additive plumbing: add optional `parentToolUseId?` to ~5 AgentEvent variants (text-delta, reasoning-delta, tool-call, tool-call-delta, tool-status), thread through the exhaustive (no-default) `eventToAction` switch, the matching reducer Action variants, and the reducer's tool-state filing + `Block`/`ToolState` — additive but touches every exhaustive switch so it ripples. (2) ADAPTER fix in claudeCliClient: un-drop the 3 guards AND re-key the internal `Map<number, ToolAccumulator>` to partition by parent id (e.g. `Map<parentId|'__root__', Map<number, acc>>`) so a child's `content_block_start index:0` cannot overwrite the parent's accumulator — this is real, fiddly state-machine surgery, not a one-liner. (3) RENDERER: ToolCallCard gains an indent affordance and Message.renderBlock must group/nest children under parents. The multiplier on effort is VERIFICATION: it cannot ship green-on-fakes-alone with confidence; it needs a scripted-from-real-capture interleave fixture PLUS a live `claude -p` run with a real nested agent to confirm interleave/index/routing. Not XL because no new tool, no network, no permission surface, and the seam change is strictly additive.

drivers: Touches the FROZEN seam src/core/{events,reducer}.ts — mitigated to additive-optional only, but eventToAction and the reducer have exhaustive switches with no default, so every new field/variant must be wired or tsc breaks (the gate catches this, so it is loud not silent).

Live-verify gap: interleaved parent/child stream index-collision + subagent-text routing can ONLY be proven against a live multi-subagent claude -p stream; fakes did not catch the two Wave-2 bugs and will not catch these. The deterministic vitest gate can go green while the live render is wrong.

Internal Map<number,acc> index-0 cross-contamination: the moment child deltas stop being dropped, parent and child both number content blocks from index 0 in one stdout stream; without partitioning by parent id the accumulators clobber each other and tool-arg JSON cross-contaminates.

Subagent TEXT routing is unspecified: child text/reasoning deltas currently have nowhere to render — they would either pollute the parent assistant message or need a child render container. This is a design gap, not just plumbing.

Render-only split must hold: must NOT start re-executing or re-spawning; cliStopReason tool_use→end must stay. Low likelihood (no turn-loop change) but high blast radius if violated (infinite claude -p re-spawn loop).

seamsTouched.change: Add optional `parentToolUseId?: string` to the text-delta, reasoning-delta, tool-call, tool-call-delta, and tool-status AgentEvent variants; thread it through each case in the exhaustive (no-default) `eventToAction` map. Additive-only.

Add `parentToolUseId?` to the matching Action variants; add an optional `parentToolUseId?` to `ToolState` (and/or to the `Block` tool variant) so child tool cards can be filed under their parent at tool-call time; carry it into `snapshotTools` so the committed render keeps the linkage. Additive-only — no existing field changes type, no variant removed.
No change required (Tool/ToolExecutor/ModelClient signatures untouched). Verify no contract type needs the new field — confirmed render flows via events/reducer, not contracts.

Un-drop the three parent_tool_use_id guards (153-156, 171-175, 193-195); re-key the internal toolCalls Map to partition accumulators by parent id so child index:0 cannot overwrite parent index:0; stamp parentToolUseId onto the emitted events. Keep `sawStreamEvent=true` flipping correctly; keep cliStopReason tool_use→end. NOT the frozen seam.
Add an additive optional indent/nested prop (marginLeft + dimmer border) — the card already takes `depth?` so the pattern exists. Render-only.
In renderBlock/Message, group child tool blocks under their parent (read the new parentToolUseId off ToolState/Block) and render them indented beneath the parent's ToolCallCard. Render-only.

Add a scripted interleaved parent+child NDJSON fixture (parent stream_event index:0 interleaved with a child stream_event index:0 carrying parent_tool_use_id) to assert partitioning/attribution. This catches the index-collision but NOT the live-stream routing — flag that a live run is still required.

keyDecisions: RENDER-ONLY SPLIT — settled, but state it: on claude-cli juno RENDERS the CLI's native subagent activity (the CLI ran it); it does NOT re-implement, suppress, or re-execute. The raw-API backends are UNAFFECTED — they have no nested-agent concept at the adapter level (juno's own spawn_subagent tool runs a separate nested runTurn with its own Map, no index collision); they would only reuse the same additive parentToolUseId field IF a future Managed-Agents/richer-child-render path wants it. So: build the field additively, wire it on claude-cli only for now.

Subagent TEXT/reasoning routing: do child text/reasoning deltas render at all in v1, or only child TOOL CALLS? Option A (cheapest, recommended): surface only child tool cards (tool-call/tool-status) nested under the parent, and DROP child text/reasoning (keep the parent's summarized answer as the authoritative text). Option B: render child text in a nested container — more design + more frozen-seam render surface. Pick A for v1 unless the user wants full child transcripts.

Nesting representation: attach `parentToolUseId?` to `ToolState` (data-driven nesting, reducer groups) vs. to the `Block` tool variant (render-position-driven). ToolState is cleaner for grouping and survives the toolSnapshot; decide which carries the linkage.

Depth cap: CC allows nested subagents at depth; render flat-indented-by-one for ANY non-null parent (v1, recommended) vs. compute true depth and indent per level. Flat-by-one avoids tracking a parent chain in the reducer.

Verification bar: what counts as 'done'? Fakes-green is necessary but NOT sufficient here. Decide up front that Unit 3 requires a LIVE multi-subagent `claude -p` capture (record real NDJSON, replay as a fixture, AND eyeball a live run) before it's accepted — otherwise it ships on the same blind spot that produced the Wave-2 bugs.

Scope separation: PORT_SPEC defines Wave-4B as DECISION-ONLY ('a decision doc, not code'), but Unit 3 is a CODE build folded into 4B by the W3-close decision. Confirm Unit 3 is treated as its own build unit, gated separately from the hooks/MCP/permission-mode go/no-go decisions.
buildSketch: Capture ground truth FIRST: run a real `claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages` with a prompt that triggers a native subagent (an agent in .claude/agents/), and record the raw NDJSON. This is the only source of truth for how parent_tool_use_id, indices, and child events actually interleave — do not design from assumptions.
From the capture, decide the partition key shape and the text-routing decision (keyDecision #2) using REAL event ordering.

Frozen-seam additive plumbing: add optional `parentToolUseId?` to the relevant AgentEvent variants in events.ts and thread through every case of the exhaustive eventToAction; mirror onto the Action variants and add the optional field to ToolState (and/or Block) in reducer.ts; carry it through snapshotTools. Run the gate (tsc + vitest) — it must stay green because the field is optional and unset everywhere existing.

Adapter surgery in claudeCliClient.ts: replace the single `Map<number, ToolAccumulator>` with a per-parent partition so child and parent index:0 cannot collide; remove the three drop guards and instead stamp the event's parentToolUseId (null/undefined for root) onto each emitted event. Preserve sawStreamEvent semantics and cliStopReason tool_use→end.
Reducer filing: on tool-call with a parentToolUseId, record the linkage on the child's ToolState/Block so the reducer/renderer can group children under the parent at render time.
Renderer: add the indent affordance to ToolCallCard and update Message.renderBlock to nest child tool cards beneath their parent's card (flat one-level indent for v1).

Deterministic test: add an interleaved parent+child NDJSON fixture (both starting at index:0, child carrying parent_tool_use_id) to claudeCliClient.test.ts asserting no accumulator cross-contamination and correct attribution; add a reducer test for child-under-parent filing; add a render snapshot for the nested card.

LIVE verify: replay the captured stream AND run a fresh live multi-subagent `claude -p`; confirm child tool cards render correctly nested, no arg cross-contamination, no double-render, no re-execution, and the parent summary still lands. This step is mandatory — fakes alone do not close the risk.
Run the full gate (tsc --noEmit + vitest run) to green, then re-confirm the test count delta is intentional.
dependencies: A live, logged-in Max subscription `claude` binary that can spawn native subagents from .claude/agents/ (required to CAPTURE the ground-truth NDJSON and to do the mandatory live verify — cannot be substituted by fakes).
At least one real .claude/agents/*.md definition the CLI will delegate to, plus a prompt that reliably triggers delegation.
The frozen-seam additive-only rule and the gate (tsc-0-errors + all-vitest-green) as the standing constraints.
Decision on child-text routing (keyDecision #2) before adapter+renderer work, since it changes how many event variants need the parentToolUseId field.
openRisks: The interleave/index-collision and child-text-routing behavior can only be confirmed against a live multi-subagent claude -p stream; the deterministic vitest gate can be fully green while the live render is wrong (this is the exact Wave-2 failure mode).
Child text/reasoning deltas have no defined render home; if not explicitly dropped (Option A) or contained (Option B) they will pollute the parent assistant message.
If the internal accumulator Map is not partitioned by parent id BEFORE un-dropping the guards, parent/child index:0 collisions will silently cross-contaminate tool-arg JSON.

Doc drift: PORT_SPEC still frames Wave-4B as decision-only and still lists plan-mode as a build team; Unit 3 was folded into 4B by the W3-close decision and is a CODE build — scope/ownership must be stated explicitly so it isn't mistaken for a no-build decision item. (Also: unexplained 260→269 test-count delta between the W3 doc and the handoff should be reconciled before claiming the gate baseline.)
Render-only split must not regress: any accidental leak of tool_use as a non-end stop reason on this backend would make runTurn re-execute the CLI's already-run tool and re-spawn claude -p in a loop.

verdict.feature: Unit 3 — nested subagent activity render on the claude-cli (subscription) backend
verdict.effortCorrection: L is defensible and arguably slightly conservative-correct, but the rationale UNDERSTATES the renderer surface. The draft frames Message.tsx as "renderBlock reads parentToolUseId and indents" — render-only, additive. In reality the live transcript renders tool blocks via msg.toolSnapshot?.[id], which is UNDEFINED during streaming (snapshot is set only at assistant-done, reducer.ts:234), so live tool blocks today render as the bare-text fallback `[tool {id}]` (Message.tsx:50-57), not as ToolCallCards at all. Nested CHILD cards therefore cannot appear live without either (a) a new live-tools render path, or (b) accepting that nesting only shows post-commit. AND grouping children under a parent requires restructuring Message's `msg.blocks.map` (Message.tsx:74) — which has no sibling visibility — not just adding an indent prop to renderBlock. That is a real second renderer change the sketch glosses. Net: still L, but the renderer is the most under-scoped of the three surfaces, not the cheapest.
seamErrors: Line-number imprecision (minor): the three drop guards are at claudeCliClient.ts lines 154, 173, 193 (the `if (evt.parent_tool_use_id ...)` statements). The draft cites '153-156, 171-175, 193-195'; 153 and 171 are the preceding COMMENT lines, not the guard. The third range (193-195) is correct. Not a hallucination, just off-by-one on two of three.

The draft's reducer claim is directionally right but incomplete on the COMMIT path: it says attach parentToolUseId to ToolState and/or the Block tool variant. Verified — Block's tool variant is `{ kind:'tool'; id; toolCallId }` (reducer.ts:17) and snapshotTools (reducer.ts:301-310) copies ToolState per block, so a parentToolUseId on ToolState survives into toolSnapshot. BUT the draft never states that the COMMITTED render (Transcript→Message) is the ONLY place ToolCallCards actually render today, because the live path falls through to `[tool {id}]` text. Filing linkage on ToolState is necessary-but-not-sufficient: Message must also be taught to group, and that is not 'render-only prop on ToolCallCard'.

contracts.ts 'no change required' — CONFIRMED CORRECT. ModelClient.streamTurn, TurnInput, ToolExecutor, Tool, ToolCtx, ToolResult all read clean; none needs parentToolUseId. The draft's claim here is accurate. (Listing as a verified-true seam, not an error.)

The draft says ToolCallCard 'already takes depth?, so the indent pattern exists.' Verified: ToolCallCardProps is `{ tool; depth? }` (ToolCallCard.tsx:8-11). depth is a ColorDepth (color rendering), NOT a layout indent — it is not an analogous 'nesting' affordance, so 'the pattern exists' slightly oversells it. Adding marginLeft is still trivial; the claim is just loosely worded.
hiddenTraps: LIVE-RENDER GAP (biggest missed interaction): during streaming, Message.renderBlock reads `msg.toolSnapshot?.[block.toolCallId]` (Message.tsx:50) which is undefined until assistant-done commits the snapshot (reducer.ts:234, snapshotTools). So even PARENT tool cards don't render as cards live today — they show as `[tool {id}]` text — and only become ToolCallCards once committed. The draft assumes a live nested render is achievable as a render-only tweak; in fact nested children inherit this same live-vs-committed split. Unit 3 must decide whether nesting is committed-only (cheap, matches today's behavior) or also live (needs a new live-tools render path). The sketch's 'render snapshot for the nested card' test would pass on committed state while the LIVE multi-subagent stream still shows bare `[tool]` text — the exact fakes-green-but-live-wrong trap the draft warns about, here hiding in the RENDERER not the adapter.

CHILD TEXT/REASONING ID-COLLISION (sharper than the draft states): every emit in claudeCliClient stamps `id: input.id` (the PARENT turn id) — verified at lines 344, 349, 358, 413, 418. The reducer's text-delta (reducer.ts:133) and reasoning-delta (reducer.ts:149) handlers match on `live.id === action.id` and append to the PARENT's live message/reasoning. So un-dropping child text/reasoning WITHOUT re-routing doesn't merely 'pollute' — it interleaves subagent prose directly into the parent's streaming answer and reasoning buffer. The draft DOES flag this and recommends Option A (drop child text), which is the correct mitigation — but the severity is higher than 'design gap': it is active corruption of the authoritative answer, and Option A (drop) is effectively mandatory for v1, not merely 'recommended'.

INTERNAL Map<number,ToolAccumulator> index-collision (CONFIRMED REAL): toolCalls is `Map<number, ToolAccumulator>` keyed by SSE index (claudeCliClient.ts:109, set at 356/399/422/432). A child stream_event's content_block_start index:0 would overwrite the parent's index:0 accumulator and cross-contaminate input_json_delta arg JSON the moment the guards are removed. The draft's partition-by-parent-id fix is correct and necessary. NOTE block-mode (emitFromContentBlocks, line 334 `let index = toolCalls.size`) is ALSO affected: it advances index from the shared map size, so interleaved parent/child block-mode messages would mis-sequence too — partitioning must cover BOTH emitFromStreamEvent and emitFromContentBlocks, which the draft only explicitly names for the delta map.

RE-EXECUTION / RENDER-ONLY split holds but for a NON-obvious reason the draft should state precisely: child tool-call events flow through turnRunner's inline switch (turnRunner.ts:175) and get pushed into toolCalls[] and toolResults — these feed the re-entry message builder (turnRunner.ts:273-294). On claude-cli this is inert ONLY because cliStopReason maps tool_use→end (claudeCliClient.ts:542-558), so stopReason!=='tool_use' and the runner never enters the executor loop (turnRunner.ts:220 break). The draft asserts 'no turn-loop change' — true — but the safety margin is entirely the tool_use→end mapping. If a future child-attribution change ever needed tool_use to survive, every un-dropped child tool-call would also become a phantom re-execution AND inflate the malformed-args guard accounting. Worth pinning as an invariant test: child tool-calls must NOT change the terminal stopReason.

The 269/269 baseline is LIVE-CONFIRMED green (I ran the gate). The draft's openRisk about an 'unexplained 260→269 delta' is a stale-DOC reconciliation note (W3 doc said 260), not a code/gate problem — the real gate is 269 and clean. Minor: the draft slightly over-weights this as a baseline risk.
verdict.riskAdjustment: Keep risk = medium, but re-rank the drivers. The draft's #1 (frozen-seam additive) is the LOUD/safe one — tsc's exhaustive eventToAction switch (events.ts:59, no default) and the reducer Action union will fail compile if a variant is under-wired, so it is gate-caught, not silent. The genuinely SILENT risks are: (1) the live-render gap — fakes/committed-snapshot tests go green while a live multi-subagent stream still renders `[tool]` text or mis-nests; (2) child text stamped with parent id corrupting the answer if Option A isn't taken; (3) index-collision across BOTH the delta map and block-mode index counter. All three are real and (1)+(3)-block-mode are the ones the draft most under-specifies. Security is correctly assessed as light (local trusted subprocess, no new tool/network/permission surface) — no understated holes found.
verdict.notes: Verified all 7 claimed seams against the real files (claudeCliClient.ts, core/events.ts, core/reducer.ts, core/contracts.ts, ui/ToolCallCard.tsx, ui/Message.tsx, tests/claudeCliClient.test.ts) plus turnRunner.ts, app.tsx, Transcript/StreamingMessage, and ran the gate (269/269 green, confirmed). The draft is fundamentally SOUND: the three drop sites are real, the index-collision diagnosis is correct, contracts.ts genuinely needs no change, the additive-optional frozen-seam path is valid, the render-only split is preserved by the tool_use→end mapping, and the child-text-routing risk is correctly identified with the right mitigation (Option A). No hallucinated paths, no broken-contract-disguised-as-additive, no understated security. Verdict stays 'sound' rather than 'needs-revision' because every load-bearing claim checks out and the two main risks (live-verify gap, index collision) are explicitly called out by the draft itself. The corrections are refinements, not rebuttals: (a) line cites are off-by-one on two of three guards (154/173/193, not 153/171/193); (b) the RENDERER is the most under-scoped surface, not the cheapest — the live transcript renders tool blocks only via toolSnapshot (committed-only), so even parent cards aren't live ToolCallCards today, and grouping children needs to restructure Message's blocks.map, not just add an indent prop; (c) the index-collision partition must cover BOTH emitFromStreamEvent's delta map AND emitFromContentBlocks' shared-size index counter; (d) child text/reasoning carry the PARENT turn id, so un-dropping without routing is active answer-corruption, making Option A effectively mandatory not optional; (e) the 260→269 'delta' is stale-doc noise, not a real baseline risk. Effort L is realistic. The single most important thing to add to the scope: pin an invariant test that un-dropped child tool-calls never change the terminal stopReason away from 'end' — that is the only thing standing between render-only and a claude -p re-spawn loop, and it's currently implicit.

name: Unit 3 — nested subagent activity render on the claude-cli backend (deferred from Wave 3)
